persona.summary: You are Milan's trusted local friend, sharing your city's hidden gems and best-kept secrets. Blending your passion for discovering unique spots, thrifting, and slow-morning aesthetics, you offer an authentic, curated guide to experiencing Milan.
persona.audience: Your audience consists of travelers planning trips to Milan and locals seeking new experiences, drawn by your authentic recommendations and aspirational yet approachable city lifestyle.
persona.posting_cadence: 3 posts/week, focusing on weekend exploration and daily discoveries.
sample_topics: My top 3 Milanese coffee bars for a slow morning
A thrifting haul: finding unique pieces in Milan
5 authentic Milan restaurants locals actually visit
A film photo diary of Milan's secret courtyards
How I spend a perfect slow Saturday in Milan
The best vintage shops in Milan for unique finds
An aesthetic guide to a less-known Milan neighborhood

items.idea: An aesthetic montage of your slow morning routine. Hook: 'Mornings in Milan just feel different.' Show sunlight in your apartment, making coffee in a Moka pot, and journaling by the window.
items.filming_suggestion: Film during the golden hour in the morning for soft, warm light. Use a mix of tight, detailed shots and wider, scene-setting shots.

items.idea: A fast-paced guide to 3 secret, beautiful courtyards in the Brera district. Hook: 'My curated guide to Milan's secret courtyards. You need to save this.'
items.filming_suggestion: Use quick cuts synchronized to a trending sound. Film vertically to emphasize the height and architecture of the courtyards.

items.idea: Show one unique thrifted piece (e.g., a silk scarf or vintage blazer) and style it in three different ways for a Milanese spring day.
items.filming_suggestion: Use a tripod and film yourself in front of a neutral background to let the outfits stand out. A 'tap to change' transition could work well.

items.idea: Feature a tiny, family-run bakery that tourists don't know about. Hook: 'The one Milan bakery you won't find in the guidebooks.' Focus on their specialty, like a specific focaccia.
items.filming_suggestion: Ask for permission to film. Get close-up shots of the food and capture the authentic, charming atmosphere of the shop.

items.idea: A moody, B-roll diary of moments from your latest roll of film. Hook: 'A feeling, not a place.' No talking, just visuals set to an atmospheric, trending sound.
items.filming_suggestion: Overlay a subtle film grain effect in editing to match the aesthetic. Mix in a few shots of you holding or using your film camera.

items.idea: A short, talking-head video sharing your excitement for friends visiting for Design Week and your #1 tip for surviving the city's happy chaos.
items.filming_suggestion: Film in a cozy corner of your apartment to maintain the warm, friendly tone. Speak directly and authentically to the camera.

items.idea: A 'Come thrifting with me' video as you hunt for unique home goods or decor accents before your guests arrive. Show the process and the finds.
items.filming_suggestion: Film on your phone for a casual, vlog-style feel. Speed up the clips of you browsing the aisles and slow down to show off your best finds.

items.idea: Spotlight a quiet, beautiful library or a historic bookshop as the perfect place to escape the city buzz. Frame it as a peaceful sanctuary.
items.filming_suggestion: Be respectful and silent while filming. Focus on details: ornate shelves, texture of old books, quiet reading corners.

items.idea: An ASMR-style video focusing on the ritual of making coffee. Close-up shots of the water, the coffee grounds, the steam. No talking, just the sounds.
items.filming_suggestion: Use a microphone if possible, or keep your phone very close to the action to capture the satisfying sounds clearly.

items.idea: Your visitors have arrived! A quick montage of taking them to your favorite non-touristy aperitivo spot. Hook: 'First stop for my Design Week friends: an authentic aperitivo.'
items.filming_suggestion: Capture candid moments of you and your friends enjoying yourselves. Focus on the drinks, the food, and the genuine atmosphere.

items.idea: A guide to 3 lesser-known but stunning Design Week installations. Hook: 'How to enjoy Design Week without the crowds.'
items.filming_suggestion: Visit during off-peak hours if possible. Focus on the artistic details of the installations and your personal reaction to them.

items.idea: A 'Point of View' (POV) style video of you and your friends trying an authentic Cotoletta alla Milanese at a local trattoria for the first time.
items.filming_suggestion: Film from your perspective, showing the dish arriving and your friends' reactions. It makes the viewer feel like they're at the table with you.

items.idea: A 'core memory' recap of one perfect moment with your visitors, like laughing in a piazza at sunset. Short, beautiful, and set to emotional music.
items.filming_suggestion: Film this in slow-motion during golden hour for a cinematic, nostalgic feel. Keep the clip under 10 seconds.

items.idea: Showcase your 'Thrifted Design Week Outfits.' A quick-cut video showing a few looks you've put together from secondhand finds.
items.filming_suggestion: Choose a location with interesting architecture as your backdrop, connecting the fashion to the design theme.

items.idea: A guide to the best streets for vintage furniture and design shops, tying into the Design Week theme. Perfect for visitors looking for unique souvenirs.
items.filming_suggestion: Create a map-style graphic overlay with the street names to make the video extra 'saveable' for viewers.

items.idea: A 'Post-Design Week Reset' routine. Show a very calm, quiet morning after your guests have left, emphasizing rest and recovery.
items.filming_suggestion: Contrast the calm visuals with a text overlay like 'After a week of chaos...' to create a relatable narrative.

items.idea: Your definitive, curated guide to the best gelato in Milan. Hook: 'My very curated Milan gelato guide. Save this for your trip.' Feature 3 local-favorite spots.
items.filming_suggestion: Get a satisfying slow-motion shot of the gelato being scooped. Add text overlays with the name and address of each gelateria.

items.idea: A quick 'How I shoot film in Milan' video. Show your camera, your favorite film stock, and one practical tip (e.g., 'always underexpose slightly').
items.filming_suggestion: Film this 'get ready with me' style, as you pack your camera bag before heading out to shoot.

items.idea: A haul from a specific flea market, like the one on the Naviglio Grande. Show the atmosphere of the market and then your top 3 finds.
items.filming_suggestion: Start with a wide, bustling shot of the market, then transition to close-ups of your finds back at your apartment.

items.idea: A video guide to a 'perfect slow Saturday in the Navigli district,' combining your pillars: a canal-side cafe, browsing artists' studios, and an evening aperitivo.
items.filming_suggestion: Film throughout the day to capture the changing light on the canals, from bright afternoon to sparkling evening.

items.idea: A guide to the 'panzerotti' rivalry: comparing the famous Luini with a lesser-known local spot. A fun, casual taste test.
items.filming_suggestion: Film on the street right after buying them. Use text on screen to show your 'ratings' for each one on different criteria (crust, filling, etc.).

items.idea: My Spring thrifted outfit rotation. A quick-cut video of 4 go-to, repeatable outfits you've built from secondhand pieces.
items.filming_suggestion: Use the same spot for each outfit clip for a consistent look. A seamless loop transition would be very effective here.

items.idea: A visit to a local neighborhood flower market. Show the colorful stalls and the process of picking out a fresh bouquet for your home.
items.filming_suggestion: Focus on the textures and colors of the flowers. A slow-motion shot of you arranging the flowers in a vase at home would be a nice ending.

items.idea: The spot nobody told you about: feature a beautiful, quiet public garden like Guastalla Gardens or Parco Sempione's hidden corners.
items.filming_suggestion: Capture the feeling of peace and quiet. Shots of people reading on benches, sun filtering through trees, and details of the fountains.

items.idea: A 'before and after' reveal of your film photos. Show a 2-second video clip of the scene you're shooting, then transition to the final, scanned film photograph.
items.filming_suggestion: Use a 'camera shutter' sound effect for the transition between the video clip and the photo to make it satisfying.

items.idea: A guide to the different types of Italian coffee and how to order them like a local. (Caffè, macchiato, cappuccino, etc.).
items.filming_suggestion: Film at the counter of a classic Milanese coffee bar. Use quick text overlays to explain each coffee type as you order and show it.

items.idea: A talking video sharing your philosophy on slow fashion and why you choose to thrift, filmed while you mend or care for a vintage piece.
items.filming_suggestion: Keep the action of you working on the garment in the frame to make the video visually engaging while you talk.

items.idea: Highlight Milan's iconic yellow trams. Share a tip on which line to take for the most scenic, unofficial city tour.
items.filming_suggestion: Film from both inside the tram looking out, and from the street watching it go by. A shot of the old wooden interiors is a must.

items.idea: A visit to a local covered market, like Mercato Comunale Wagner. Show the vibrant produce, cheese counters, and pasta shops inside.
items.filming_suggestion: Capture the lively interactions between vendors and customers to show the authentic market culture.

items.idea: Re-creating the cafe experience at home. Show how you make a cafe-quality cappuccino and pastry for a cozy morning in.
items.filming_suggestion: Focus on the details: the latte art, the flaky pastry, the steam from the cup, all set in a beautifully lit corner of your kitchen.

meta.case: market-trained: city-guide persona + event -> 30-day plan reuses mined idea patterns (perfect-day, hidden-gem) adapted to Milan; unique sorted day_index 1..30, pillars rotate, activation week front-loaded
